rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).
Source URL + type label — mandatory. Scope: maintainer-block only, not student-facing body.
Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Willison and Ronacher are named as supporting sources in the maintainer block without specific URLs — add URLs if available to fully satisfy §11's 'every URL to open against the original' requirement. Non-blocking: they are supplementary context, not load-bearing body claims.

platform_claims_checked.claim: I'm keeping two Claude windows open. One making changes to the source code. The other helping me to plan future changes.
platform_claims_checked.note: Practitioner blockquote (Uncle Bob's own words) — not a curriculum capability assertion. No §4 verify-before-assert trigger.

platform_claims_checked.claim: His learning journey starts with obvious excitement. AI can write code. A lot of code. Fast.
platform_claims_checked.note: General observation framing, not a Claude Code capability claim.

citations_checked.claim: Uncle Bob on keeping two Claude windows open for planning vs. implementation
citations_checked.note: Label correctly absent from student-facing body per §1 scope rule. Body uses builder voice. Maintainer block carries provenance.

citations_checked.note: Same — label correctly absent from body.
